sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 7.7 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.7)

statement.latex: (Well-known problem). Is the group$G= < a,b a^9=1,\;\; ab= b^2a^2 >$finite? This group contains$F(2, 9)$, the only Fibonacci group for which it is not yet known whether it is finite or infinite. { R.\,G.\,Burns} No, it is infinite, since$F(2,9)$is infinite (M.\,F.\,Newman, Arch. Math., { 54}, no.\,3 (1990), 209--212).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
